{"step_by_step_thinking": "Helmet cells are a specific abnormal red blood cell morphology observed in microangiopathic hemolytic anemias. According to the MEDCORP document (Document [0]), in conditions characterized by microangiopathic hemolysis—such as thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP), hemolytic uremic syndrome (HUS), and disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC)—a peripheral blood smear may reveal helmet cells, fragmented RBCs (schistocytes), and other RBC fragments. This directly links helmet cells to microangiopathic hemolytic anemia. Hemolytic uremic syndrome (HUS) is explicitly mentioned as one of the conditions associated with microangiopathic hemolysis and the presence of schistocytes and helmet cells. Spherocytosis (Option C) is associated with spherocytes, not helmet cells. Acanthocytosis (Option D) is associated with spur cells, not helmet cells. Polysplenia (Option B) is not mentioned in any of the documents in relation to helmet cells. Therefore, the correct answer is Hemolytic uremic syndrome.", "answer_choice": "A"}
